sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 힐튼, 2025년 3분기 RevPAR 1.1% 하락에도 조정 EBITDA 9억 7,600만 달러로 견조한 수익성 유지

keyPoints.text: 3분기 시스템 전체 비교 RevPAR이 전년 동기 대비 1.1% 감소했으나, 희석 EPS 1.78달러·조정 희석 EPS 2.11달러를 달성했습니다.
3분기 관리·프랜차이즈 수수료 매출이 전년 동기 대비 5.3% 증가했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 3분기 RevPAR이 점유율과 ADR 소폭 하락으로 1.1% 감소하며 업계 RevPAR 연성화 국면이 확인됐습니다.

businessChanges.text: 힐튼이 Outset Collection by Hilton 신규 라이프스타일 브랜드를 론칭하고, 시그니아 by 힐튼 라칸테라 리조트로 9,000번째 프로퍼티 마일스톤을 달성했습니다.
3분기 전년 동기 대비 순 유닛 성장률 6.5%를 달성하며 파이프라인이 전년 대비 5% 증가했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 연간 순이익 가이던스는 16억 400만 달러~16억 2,500만 달러, 조정 EBITDA는 36억 8,500만 달러~37억 1,500만 달러입니다.
2025년 연간 자본환원 계획은 약 33억 달러이며 3분기까지 26억 7,100만 달러가 집행됐습니다.
2025년 연간 순 유닛 성장률 목표는 6.5%~7.0%로 유지됐습니다.

watchItems.text: RevPAR이 1.1% 하락했음에도 수수료 매출 5.3% 성장과 조정 EBITDA 유지로 asset-light 모델의 방어력이 확인됐습니다.
RevPAR 회복 속도와 2026년 가이던스 수준이 추후 성장 모멘텀의 핵심 지표가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 q32025earningsrelease.htm PRESS RELEASE Document Investor Contact 7930 Jones Branch Drive Charlie Ruehr McLean, VA 22102 +1 703 883 1000 ir.hilton.com Media Contact Kent Landers +1 703 883 3246 Hilton Reports Third Quarter Results MCLEAN, VA (October 22, 2025) - Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. ("Hilton," "the Company," "we," "us" or "our") (NYSE: HLT) today reported its third quarter 2025 results. Highlights include: • Diluted EPS was $1.78 for the third quarter, and diluted EPS, adjusted for special items, was $2.11 • Net income was $421 million for the third quarter • Adjusted EBITDA was $976 million for the third quarter • System-wide comparable RevPAR declined 1.1 percent, on a currency neutral basis, for the third quarter compared to the same period in 2024 • Approved 33,000 new  Normalized numeric aliases: $421 million = 4억 2,100만 달러; $976 million = 9억 7,600만 달러; $1.78 = 1.78달러; $2.11 = 2.11달러; $421 = 421달러; $976 = 976달러; 33,000 million = 330억 달러; 1.1 percent = 1.1%.

rooms as of September 30, 2025, representing growth of 5 percent from September 30, 2024 • Added 24,800 rooms to our system, resulting in 23,200 net additional rooms for the third quarter, contributing to net unit growth of 6.5 percent from September 30, 2024 • Announced the launch of a new lifestyle brand, Outset Collection by Hilton, in October 2025 • In October 2025, reached our 9,000th property milestone with the opening of the Signia by Hilton La Cantera Resort and Spa • Repurchased 2.8 million shares of Hilton common stock during the third quarter, bringing total capital return, including dividends, to $792 million for the quarter and $2,671 million year to date through October  Normalized numeric aliases: 2.8 million = 280만 달러; $792 million = 7억 9,200만 달러; $2,671 million = 26억 7,100만 달러; $792 = 792달러; $2,671 = 2,671달러; 24,800 million = 248억 달러; 23,200 million = 232억 달러; 9,000 million = 90억 달러; 5 percent = 5%; 2,671 million = 26억 7,100만 달러; 6.5 percent = 6.5%; 1.0 percent = 1.0%.

$1,604 million and $1,625 million; full year Adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $3,685 million and $3,715 million • Full year 2025 capital return is projected to be approximately $3.3 billion 1 Overview Christopher J. Nassetta, President & Chief Executive Officer of Hilton, said, "Our third quarter results continued to demonstrate the resilience of our business model, delivering strong bottom line performance despite softer industry RevPAR. We remain optimistic, that in the U.S., lower interest rates, a more favorable regulatory environment, certainty on tax policy and a significant investment cycle will accelerate economic growth and travel demand, and, when paired with limited industry supply growth, should drive  Normalized numeric aliases: $1,604 million = 16억 400만 달러; $1,625 million = 16억 2,500만 달러; $3,685 million = 36억 8,500만 달러; $3,715 million = 37억 1,500만 달러; $3.3 billion = 33억 달러; $1,604 = 1,604달러; $1,625 = 1,625달러; $3,685 = 3,685달러; $3.3 = 3.3달러; $3,715 = 3,715달러.

our brands for conversions and continued growth of our brand presence globally gives us confidence in delivering net unit growth between 6.5 percent and 7.0 percent in 2025 and 6.0 percent to 7.0 percent over the next several years." For the three months ended September 30, 2025, system-wide comparable RevPAR decreased 1.1 percent compared to the same period in 2024 due to modest occupancy and ADR declines. Management and franchise fee revenues increased 5.3 percent compared to the same period in 2024. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, system-wide comparable RevPAR increased 0.3 percent compared to the same period in 2024 due to an increase in ADR. Management and franchise fee revenues increased 6.1 percent compared to the same period in 2024.
